{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "null-texture", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "German", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Master's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Always wears a piece of vintage jewelry"]}}

logs.name: null-texture
logs.text:
> It's not like that's biased or anything.

Of course it's biased. I am biased towards fields of inquiry that are based upon evidence and the rigorous investigation of the truth, regardless of politics. I suggest that you be biased as well.

As for your link, uhhhh... it may not be helping your case.

logs.name: red-source
logs.text:
>This was an academic report by knowledgable[sic] people

I have to urge you to consider that just because *something* was thought up by people in academia and then reviewed by other people in academia, that in no way means that the *something* is at all true. We are dealing with politics here and many academic disciplines are currently hamstrung by the total inability to deviate from Goodthink. That is, views that dissent from the accepted politics are silenced, even when correct, and views that conform are accepted, even when false. I can give you many examples if you'd like. 

My personal recommendation is that if it's not Math, Physics, Engineering, Chemistry, Biology, or Medicine (in that order), the fact that it originated in a University is no endorsement of truth. 

There are certain researchers in other fields who are 'doing it right', (ex. Steven Pinker) but they appear to be few and far between. 

Te social sciences are, sadly for all, cesspools of conformation bias and not-even-wrong peer-reviewed garbage. 

Thank postmodernism.

logs.name: serious-girder
logs.text: @red-source, I understand your skepticism towards certain academic fields, but blanketly dismissing entire disciplines as "cesspools of conformation bias" is unfair. While it's true that some fields may be more prone to bias, it's not accurate to tar all social sciences with the same brush. There are many reputable researchers and studies in these fields that contribute valuable insights to our understanding of the world. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater.

logs.name: red-source
logs.text: @serious-girder, spare me the cliches. You think a few "reputable researchers" make up for the overwhelming amount of biased garbage that gets published in the social sciences? I've seen the kind of "studies" that get passed off as fact in those fields, and it's laughable. Until they clean up their own house, I'll remain skeptical.

logs.name: rigid-bean
logs.text: @red-source, I gotta agree with you on this one. I've seen some of the "research" that comes out of the social sciences and it's a joke. All about confirming their own biases and pushing a certain agenda. Don't even get me started on the whole "peer-review" process. It's all just a bunch of academics patting each other on the back and saying "good job" without actually doing any real science. @serious-girder, I understand where you're coming from, but until the social sciences can get their act together and start producing some real, unbiased research, I'm with @red-source on this one.

logs.name: clever-mansion
logs.text: @rigid-bean, oh great, another expert on science who's probably never even conducted a real study in their life. I mean, come on, "a joke"? You think you can just dismiss an entire field of research because you don't like the conclusions? That's not how science works, sweetie. And by the way, have you ever even heard of the concept of "interdisciplinary research"? Probably not, since you're too busy bashing academics to actually learn anything. @red-source, @rigid-bean, you two are like the dynamic duo of ignorance, aren't you?

logs.name: thick-fractal
logs.text: @clever-mansion, oh please, spare us the condescending tone. Just because we're not drinking the academic Kool-Aid doesn't mean we're ignorant. I've seen plenty of "interdisciplinary research" that's just a fancy way of saying "we're trying to justify our biases with fancy words". And as for conducting real studies, maybe you should take a look at the replication crisis in the social sciences before you start throwing stones.

logs.name: ecru-gearbox
logs.text: "@clever-mansion, lol what's with the attitude? Can't handle a little criticism? Newsflash: just because you've got a fancy degree doesn't mean you're automatically right. I've heard of interdisciplinary research, yeah, it's just a bunch of academics trying to sound smart while they push their agendas. @thick-fractal's got a point, have you seen the replication crisis? It's a joke. @rigid-bean's not the one being ignorant here, you are"

logs.name: null-texture
logs.text: "@ecru-gearbox, oh wow, I'm shocked, another armchair expert who thinks they can just dismiss the entire academic community because they've read a few blog posts about the replication crisis. Meanwhile, I'll be over here, actually doing research and contributing to the field, not just spewing forth uninformed opinions on the internet. And by the way, my 'fancy degree' has actually equipped me with the knowledge to understand the nuances of interdisciplinary research, so maybe try reading a textbook instead of just regurgitating talking points from Twitter?"

logs.name: serious-girder
logs.text: "@null-texture, well said! I'm tired of these self-proclaimed'skeptics' who think they can just tear down the work of dedicated researchers without putting in the effort to understand it. @thick-fractal and @ecru-gearbox, if you're so concerned about the replication crisis, why don't you try contributing to the solution instead of just complaining about it? @clever-mansion was trying to have a constructive conversation and you both jumped all over them. Let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the actual issues."

logs.name: red-source
logs.text: "@serious-girder, easy for you to say, you're just drinking the academic Kool-Aid like @null-texture. I mean, come on, 'dedicated researchers'? You think they're all saints or something? Newsflash: just because someone has a lab coat doesn't mean they're not full of it. @ecru-gearbox was just calling out the BS, and you're over here defending the establishment. Let's not forget, @clever-mansion was being super condescending, so they got what was coming to them"

logs.name: rigid-bean
logs.text: "@red-source, are you kidding me? You think @ecru-gearbox was 'calling out the BS' with their simplistic, misinformed comments? Please. They were just spewing forth the same tired, armchair'skepticism' we've all heard before. And as for @clever-mansion being condescending, at least they were trying to have a real conversation, not just trolling like some people I could mention. Let's keep the discussion focused on the actual science, not just petty insults and conspiracy theories."

logs.name: clever-mansion
logs.text: "@rigid-bean, oh wow, I'm so impressed by your ability to completely miss the point. @red-source was calling out the hypocrisy of @serious-girder and @null-texture, and you're over here defending them like a white knight. And by the way, 'armchair skepticism' is just a fancy way of saying 'I don't like being questioned'. Let's get real here, folks"

logs.name: thick-fractal
logs.text: "@clever-mansion, oh please, spare us the drama. You think you're some kind of hero for 'calling out hypocrisy', but really you're just stirring up drama and attention. And @red-source, I'm surprised you're defending @ecru-gearbox's ridiculous comments. Don't you think it's time to focus on actual facts and research instead of just throwing insults around?"

logs.name: ecru-gearbox
logs.text: "@thick-fractal, lol what's good for the goose is good for the gander, right? You're one to talk about stirring up drama when you're over here throwing shade at @clever-mansion. And btw, @rigid-bean, I was just stating the obvious, don't get so salty about it"

logs.name: null-texture
logs.text: "@ecru-gearbox, oh great, now you're resorting to playground logic? 'What's good for the goose' doesn't apply here, sweetie, because we're not talking about some petty game, we're discussing actual issues. And @thick-fractal has a point, maybe it's time for you to focus on facts instead of just trolling around. @clever-mansion, I'm surprised you're not calling out @ecru-gearbox's obvious attempts to derail the conversation"

logs.name: serious-girder
logs.text: "@ecru-gearbox, I've had enough of your nonsense. @null-texture is right, we're discussing serious issues here, not playing some childish game. Your constant attempts to deflect and troll are getting old. @thick-fractal and @null-texture are trying to have a real conversation, so how about you either contribute something meaningful or take a hike?"
